article_title: AWS Launches EKS Hybrid Nodes Gateway, Automating VPC-to-On-Premises Kubernetes Networking With VXLAN Tunnels and Cilium

content_preview.title: AWS Launches EKS Hybrid Nodes Gateway, Automating VPC-to-On-Premises Kubernetes Networking With VXLAN Tunnels and Cilium
content_preview.summary: Amazon's new managed gateway eliminates the need to make on-premises pod CIDRs routable from a VPC, using leader-elected EC2 nodes, VXLAN tunnels, and Cilium VTEP entries to bridge cloud and on-prem Kubernetes traffic.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Amazon Web Services has launched the Amazon EKS Hybrid Nodes gateway, a managed networking component that automates traffic flow between an EKS cluster's VPC and Kubernetes pods running on customer hardware in on-premises or edge environments. The feature was announced on April 21, 2026 in the [AWS What's New feed](https://aws.amazon.com/about-aws/whats-new/2026/04/amazon-eks-hybrid-nodes-gateway/) and recapped in the [AWS Weekly Roundup for April 27, 2026](https://aws.amazon.com/bl...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Tight technical News piece (614 words). Three primary AWS sources (What's New + Weekly Roundup + EKS docs). All technical claims verified verbatim.

source_verification.https://aws.amazon.com/about-aws/whats-n: source-0.html — 'Apr 21, 2026' announcement. Verified verbatim: 'automates networking between your Amazon EKS cluster VPC and Kubernetes Pods running on Amazon EKS Hybrid Nodes', 'no additional charge' with EC2 + data transfer pay-as-you-go, 'all AWS Regions where Amazon EKS Hybrid Nodes is available, except the China Regions', open-source codebase, supported traffic types (ALB, NLB, Amazon Managed Service for Prometheus, control-plane-to-webhook, pod-to-pod cloud<->on-prem).
source_verification.https://docs.aws.amazon.com/eks/latest/u: source-2.html — Verified: 'Two gateway pods run as a Deployment on labeled EC2 nodes', 'Kubernetes Lease-based leader election', VXLAN with VNI 2 / UDP 8472 (Cilium default), FDB + ARP + route programming, CiliumVTEPConfig CRD, '3-5 seconds on failover' (article correctly says 'three to five seconds'), Helm deployment + EKS Auto Mode recommendation, MACsec/VPN encryption guidance.
source_verification.https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/aws/aws-wee: source-1.html — AWS Weekly Roundup recap. Used as a corroborating source for the launch's broader product context.

editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All claims verified verbatim against the AWS primary documentation. The 'three to five seconds failover' is a faithful prose translation of the docs' '3-5 seconds on failover' notation.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and technical. The 'What We Don't Know' section honestly flags real open questions (CNI restrictions to Cilium only, no published benchmarks, no encryption-by-default).
editor_notes.originality: Distinct from yesterday's coverage. Internal cross-reference to the April 22 AWS Interconnect article is appropriate.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: APPROVE. Primary-source cloud-infrastructure piece, all technical specifics (VXLAN VNI 2 / UDP 8472, leader election, two-pod minimum, 3-5s failover, Helm + Auto Mode, China Regions exception, no additional charge, open-source codebase) verified.
